name: Alange Reservoir Loop via La Zarza
name_original: Guareña - Embalse de Alange — circular por La Zarza
description: Cycle 38.1 miles (61.2 km) around the Embalse de Alange on this moderate road cycling route, enjoying Extremaduran landscapes and reservoir
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Guareña traverse diverse Extremaduran landscapes, characterized by a continuous up and down profile. The region offers a mix of flat and undulating terrain, with opportunities to cycle along the banks of the Guadiana River. Cyclists can explore dehesa landscapes and forests of holm oaks, providing varied scenery for rides. Best road cycling routes around Guareña The most popular road cycling route is Alange Reservoir Loop, a 43.2 miles (69.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete, featuring a moderate difficulty grade. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alange Reservoir Loop via La Zarza, a moderate 38.1 miles (61.2 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding Extremaduran terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Views of Medellín Castle – Medellín Roman Bridge loop from Santa Amalia, a 26.7 miles (43.0 km) trail leading through historical landmarks, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Road cycling around Guareña is defined by expansive Extremaduran landscapes, the Guadiana River, and dehesa forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging moderate routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Guareña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Guareña?
A: The road cycling routes around Guareña offer a diverse Extremaduran landscape, characterized by a continuous 'up and down' profile. You'll encounter a mix of flat sections and undulating terrain, with opportunities to cycle along the banks of the Guadiana River and through dehesa landscapes and forests of holm oaks.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or more experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, the routes around Guareña cater to various skill levels. There are 4 easy routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride, and 4 moderate routes for cyclists looking for a bit more challenge. There are no routes classified as difficult in this guide.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling near Guareña?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Medellín Castle – Medellín Roman Bridge loop from Santa Amalia&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5725452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Theater and Castle of Medellín&lt;/a&gt; and the historic Puente de Felipe IV. You can also find the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt; within the nearby Cornalvo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that feature lakes or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993709872/alange-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995549030/alange-reservoir-loop-via-la-zarza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir Loop via La Zarza&lt;/a&gt; both offer beautiful views of the Alange Reservoir. The region is also home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6059030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Muelas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, providing picturesque settings for your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Guareña?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, Extremadura generally experiences hot summers. For comfortable road cycling, spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are typically ideal, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any loop routes available for road cyclists?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Guareña are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993709872/alange-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Medellín Castle – Medellín Roman Bridge loop from Santa Amalia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical distance and duration for road cycling routes in this area?
A: The routes vary in length and time. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Medellín Castle – Medellín Roman Bridge loop from Santa Amalia&lt;/a&gt; is about 43 km (26.7 miles) and takes around 1 hour 41 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993709872/alange-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 69.6 km (43.2 miles) and can take over 3 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through historical towns or villages?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Routes often connect or pass near towns with historical significance. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Medellín Castle – Medellín Roman Bridge loop from Santa Amalia&lt;/a&gt; starts near Santa Amalia and leads to Medellín, known for its archaeological sites and castle.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Guadiana River?
A: Many routes in the Guareña area provide opportunities to cycle along or near the Guadiana River. The region's landscape is influenced by the river, and you can expect scenic stretches along its banks, offering great views and photo opportunities.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Guareña in total?
A: There are 8 road cycling routes detailed in this guide for the Guareña region, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels for exploration.

Q: Are there any routes that are particularly scenic or offer great viewpoints?
A: The entire region is known for its expansive Extremaduran landscapes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995549030/alange-reservoir-loop-via-la-zarza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir Loop via La Zarza&lt;/a&gt; offer continuous views of the reservoir and the surrounding terrain. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Medellín Castle&lt;/a&gt; highlight provides a fantastic vantage point.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while cycling?
A: Beyond the undulating terrain and riverbanks, you'll encounter the characteristic dehesa landscapes, which are open woodlands of holm oaks and cork oaks. The nearby Cornalvo Natural Park also offers a rich natural setting with its reservoir and diverse flora and fauna.
tips: La Zarza is a town located on the plain of the Guadiana River, in a geographical area where agricultural areas predominate with the Sierra de Juan Bueno and Peñas Blancas as background walls.

Arriving in the town of Alange and with the Alange Reservoir at its feet, from a distance, you can see the center with the hill where the Culebra Castle resides. This elevated construction is visible above the rest of the territory.
Going towards the town of Palomas you will find a series of agricultural fields that abound around the road. Depending on the time of year, the fields are an intense green, forming a spectacle.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Guarena - Embalse de Alange cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, as a circular route connecting Guareña, La Zarza, and Alange, you can typically find parking within these towns to begin your ride. Consider starting in Guareña or La Zarza for convenient access.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. You can expect generally easy riding conditions, suitable for a road cycling experience in Extremadura.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Extremadura can get very hot in summer. For the most comfortable experience, consider cycling during the spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this route requires a good level of fitness due to its 61.2-kilometer distance and approximately 520 meters of elevation gain. While it features rolling hills rather than sustained steep climbs, it might be challenging for absolute beginners. Experienced road cyclists will find it a manageable challenge.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle the Guarena - Embalse de Alange — circular por La Zarza route. It is freely accessible.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and natural features along the route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily circles the Embalse de Alange, offering continuous views of this significant reservoir. You'll also pass through the town of La Zarza and can enjoy Views of Alange. The route also features Fields of crops near Palomas, showcasing typical Extremaduran landscapes.

faqs.question: Is the Guarena - Embalse de Alange — circular por La Zarza route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on public roads and trails in Spain, this is a road cycling route. It's best to consider if your dog can safely accompany you for 61.2 kilometers on paved surfaces, potentially near traffic, or if it's more suitable for a solo ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, this 61.2-kilometer road cycling route takes approximately 3 hours and 1 minute to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official paths. You will encounter parts of the Camino Natural del Guadiana and the Cañada Real Leonesa Occidental, as well as smaller segments of the Camino del Cancho de los Enamorados and Camino del Encinar.

faqs.question: Are there good viewpoints along the Embalse de Alange section?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route circles the Embalse de Alange, you'll have continuous scenic views of the reservoir. The highlight Views of Alange specifically points out a notable vantage point.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find amenities near the route?
faqs.answer: The route connects the towns of Guareña, La Zarza, and Alange. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities for refreshments and supplies during or after your ride.
